title: All the elements of an aggregate should be provided with an initial value
description: All the elements of an aggregate should be provided with an initial valueAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhen an array or an object of aggregate type is non-zero initialized, values for each element or field should be provided.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoIn C or C++, it is possible to provide an initial value for the elements of an array. When fewer values are provided than the size of the array, the last elements of the array are zero-initialized for builtin-types (likeintor pointers), andvalue-initializedotherwise. However, as soon as some values are provided, it is clearer to provide them all and not rely on these default initializations.int a1[5] = {1, 2, 3}; // Noncompliant, last two elements are initialized with 0 int a2[4] = {1
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: int a1[5] = {1, 2, 3}; // Noncompliant: last two elements are initialized with 0
int* a3[3] = {ptr1, ptr2}; // Noncompliant: the last pointer is null
compliant: int a2[5] = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}; // Compliant: all elements explicitly initialized
int* a4[3] = {ptr1, ptr2, nullptr}; // Compliant: all elements explicitly initialized

checks.message: Provide explicit initial values for all elements of the aggregate to improve code clarity
